title: Podman 2:3.1.0-0.2.rc2.fc34 fails to create overlays
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Fresh from the Fedora 34 updates-testing tree podman 2:3.1.0-0.2.rc2.fc34 fails to handle overlays correctly, atleast thats what the log is related to:

```
ERRO[0010] error unmounting /home/tobias/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/bce02a1cef702ccbbd0cee9e8d1ba82c18063ef6792b26486b4bf90219769957/merged: invalid argument 
DEBU[0010] failed to mount container "831376d814f4129ea32b6af53608b7edd096ce4063d312fbffcd30eb2622a5d9": error creating overlay mount to /home/tobias/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/bce02a1cef702ccbbd0cee9e8d1ba82c18063ef6792b26486b4bf90219769957/merged, mount_data=",lowerdir=/home/tobias/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/XRR7QQFBMHO2P6EOVN2TD73LP3,upperdir=/home/tobias/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/bce02a1cef702ccbbd0cee9e8d1ba82c18063ef6792b26486b4bf90219769957/diff,workdir=/home/tobias/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/bce02a1cef702ccbbd0cee9e8d1ba82c18063ef6792b26486b4bf90219769957/work,userxattr,context=\"system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c331,c884\"": permission denied 
```

Might be related to:
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9507

Downgrading to 
podman-2:3.0.1-1.fc34.x86_64
Immediately fixes the issue

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run Fedora 34 beta with updates-testing enabled

2. `podman run -it --rm --security-opt label=disable -v local_dir:/destination fedora:35`

**Describe the results you received:**
podman fails to run due to overlay-related issues

**Describe the results you expected:**
podman to run

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes, the latest version is what breaks after all

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Fedora 34 beta on Kernel 5.11.9

comments.body: I believe this is already fixed in c/storage upstream. We're trying to get a fresh version vendored to fix before we cut 3.1.0 final.

Is this the same issue as https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1943321 / Maybe https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/841

If so it can be worked around in the mean time by creating`$HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf`.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This has been mixed in the meantime.
